records.ety: [F., fr. L. mortificatio a killing. See Mortify.]

defs: The act of mortifying, or the condition of being mortified
The death of one part of an animal body, while the rest continues to live; loss of vitality in some part of a living animal; gangrene.
Subjection of the passions and appetites, by penance, abstinence, or painful severities inflicted on the body.
quotes: The mortification of our lusts has something in it that is troublesome, yet nothing that is unreasonable.

defs: Deep humiliation or shame, from a loss of pride; painful embarassment, usually arising from exposure of a mistake; chagrin; vexation.
That which mortifies; the cause of humiliation, chagrin, or vexation.
quotes: It is one of the vexatious mortifications of a studious man to have his thoughts discovered by a tedious visit.

defs: A gift to some charitable or religious institution; -- nearly synonymous with mortmain.
